source_policy: Current official Christenson model information and governed canonical profiles are the authority. Public family reflects the model's intended design role. Board DNA describes behaviour but does not determine identity.

models.manufacturer_intent: Performance-oriented evolution of Christenson's classic fish with a slimmer nose and more vertical pocket-surfing capability.
models.editorial_reason: Fish design intent remains primary despite the stronger performance bias.

models.manufacturer_intent: Performance-minded everyday shortboard combining low rails, spiral vee, double concave and updated rocker and tail curves.
models.editorial_reason: Its core role is broad everyday performance rather than specialist competition or step-up surfing.

models.manufacturer_intent: Narrower, refined performance board for stronger, faster and more consequential surf.
models.editorial_reason: Powerful-wave hold and control are the primary design purpose.

models.manufacturer_intent: Easy-speed, forgiving small-wave design with broad planing area and accessible performance.
models.editorial_reason: Weak-wave ease and wave-count intent are stronger than its all-round daily-driver role.

models.manufacturer_intent: Traditional twin-fin fish focused on speed, flow, trim and timeless fish lines.
models.editorial_reason: This is the reference traditional fish in the Christenson range.

models.manufacturer_intent: Alternative mid-length platform focused on trim, speed, glide and rail-driven surfing.
models.editorial_reason: Longer rail line and mid-length surfing style are fundamental to the design.

models.manufacturer_intent: Full-throttle performance twin built for speed, drive and quality-wave surfing rather than retro fish lines.
models.editorial_reason: Performance shortboard intent is primary; twin-fin configuration does not make it a fish.

models.manufacturer_intent: Swallow-tail version of the Lane Splitter performance twin for speed from beach breaks through Indonesian reefs.
models.editorial_reason: Quality-wave performance and control remain primary.

models.manufacturer_intent: Long-railed performance fish designed for high lines, speed, trim and accelerating turns across a broad wave range.
models.editorial_reason: Its length, glide and rail-line role make Mid Length the clearest public family while fish identity remains visible in the subtype.

models.manufacturer_intent: Performance shortboard within the OP progression, governed as the more technical and quality-wave-oriented end of the range.
models.editorial_reason: Current canonical evidence supports Performance Shortboard and contains no credible fish, mid-length or daily-driver identity.
models.review_note: Current governed source lacks a complete official manufacturer description. Family is retained from the OP range evidence pending a stronger official source.

models.manufacturer_intent: Performance shortboard with a somewhat broader, more accessible operating range than the most specialised OP variants.
models.editorial_reason: Performance shortboard remains the primary identity even where everyday usability improves.

models.manufacturer_intent: Performance shortboard with stronger-wave and step-up relevance.
models.editorial_reason: Its primary identity remains a performance shortboard; step-up capability is secondary and it is never a fish.
models.review_note: Current governed source lacks a complete official manufacturer description. Family is retained from the OP range evidence pending a stronger official source.

models.manufacturer_intent: Refined performance mid-length for stronger waves, longer lines and controlled rail surfing.
models.editorial_reason: Mid-length rail line, paddle and glide remain fundamental despite its performance focus.

models.manufacturer_intent: Fast, drive-oriented performance mid-length with twin-fin and stronger-wave capability.
models.editorial_reason: The board's longer rail line and performance mid-length role are primary; twin and step-up traits are secondary.
